title: ARPA-H Awards $144M Across 7 Teams for PROSPR Healthy Aging Initiative
description: Federal government awards up to $144M in five-year contracts to universities and research teams studying age-related illnesses through the PROSPR program, including first Phase 3 trial targeting aging itself.

title: First Phase 3 Aging Trial: Rapamycin, SGLT2i, and Semaglutide Selected for VITAL-H Trial
description: UT Health San Antonio launches the first Phase 3 clinical trial targeting aging in healthy adults 60-65, testing rapamycin, dapagliflozin, and semaglutide with up to $38M ARPA-H funding.

title: Brown University Wins $22M ARPA-H Award to Test HIV Drug Censavudine for Slowing Aging
description: Multi-institution trial will test whether Censavudine can reduce DNA-triggered inflammation to slow aging in 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: FDA Clears Life Biosciences ER-100 Epigenetic Reprogramming for Phase 1 Human Trial
description: First partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy cleared for human trials, using OCT-4, SOX-2, and KLF-4 transcription factors to reverse cellular aging in eye disease patients.

title: Cambrian Bio Awarded $30.8M ARPA-H PROSPR Funding for mTORC1-Selective Inhibitor
description: Cambrian Bio receives $30.8M to test a next-gen mTORC1-selective inhibitor for preserving intrinsic capacity in older adults, part of the broader PROSPR initiative.

title: NIH: Manipulating Transcription Factors Reverses Aging in Mice
description: UCSF researchers identified four transcription factors (EZH2, E2F3, STAT3, ZFX) that reversed age-related gene changes in fibroblasts and improved health in old mice.

title: Shingles Vaccine May Slow Biological Aging and Reduce Inflammation
description: Study of 3,800+ Americans age 70+ found shingles vaccine recipients showed slower biological aging, suggesting vaccines may have broader anti-aging effects.

title: Popular Brain Supplement Tyrosine Linked to Shorter Lifespan in Men
description: Study of 270,000+ people found higher levels of tyrosine, a common amino acid supplement, linked to shorter lifespan in men.

title: Longeveron Phase 2b Stem Cell Therapy for Frailty Published in Cell Stem Cell
description: Phase 2b results show stem cell therapy improved physical function in patients with age-related frailty, published in Cell Stem Cell.

title: Anti-Aging Market Targeting $420B; Klothonova Advances Alpha-Klotho Production
description: Anti-aging market projected to surpass $420B by 2030. Klothonova achieves GMP production milestone for alpha-Klotho longevity protein therapeutic development.

title: Insilico Medicine and Liquid AI Launch Secure AI for Drug Research
description: Insilico Medicine partners with Liquid AI to launch a secure, lightweight AI system to streamline drug research and development pipelines.

title: Immunis Secures US/EU Rights for Parkinson's Dyskinesia Drug TRK-820
description: Exclusive licensing deal gives Immunis rights to advance TRK-820 for L-dopa-induced dyskinesia in Parkinson's patients across US and Europe.

title: Circadian Disruption Linked to Inflammatory Microglia; EV Therapy Funded
description: Animal research connects circadian disruption with inflammatory brain microglia, while new funding explores extracellular vesicle therapy for neurodegeneration.

title: AI Platform Combines Blood Biomarkers and Lifestyle Data for Personalized Aging Insights
description: New platform uses blood biomarkers, lifestyle data, and AI to give individuals personalized insights on aging rate and chronic disease risk.

title: WHOOP Joins $34.5M Initiative to Develop FDA-Grade Intrinsic Capacity Score
description: Wearable company WHOOP partners in $34.5M effort to develop the first FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity score to predict healthspan using wearable data.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytics, NK Cells, and SIRT6
description: Monthly roundup covering mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness, NK cell restoration, SIRT6 brain protection, and AI diagnostic breakthroughs.

title: Buntanetap Late-Stage Trial Advances Toward FDA Milestone for Alzheimer's
description: Late-stage buntanetap clinical trial progresses toward FDA regulatory milestone in effort to slow Alzheimer's and dementia progression.

title: Buck Institute Launches Federated Healthspan Data Initiative
description: Buck Institute launches federated data initiative linking everyday health signals with aging biology to detect disease earlier across populations.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Ultra-Processed Foods in Children Worsen Behavioral Outcomes
description: Research shared by Rhonda Patrick shows children eating more UPFs at age 3 had higher anxiety, depression, and aggression by age 5. Replacing 10% of UPFs improved scores.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Diet Quality Directly Shapes Sleep Architecture
description: Higher-fiber, plant-diverse days yield more deep and REM sleep. Processed foods and saturated fat increase nighttime wakefulness and reduce sleep quality.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Visceral Fat Increases Cancer Risk by 44%
description: Higher visceral fat linked to 44% increased risk of colon, pancreatic, and liver cancers. People with high abdominal fat ~2x more likely to die early.

title: Stanford Hosting Valter Longo on Fasting-Mimicking Diets, March 11
description: Stanford Medicine announces event with USC gerontology professor Valter Longo discussing fasting-mimicking diets and longevity research.

title: UConn Center on Aging Joins National ARPA-H Aging Research Consortium
description: UConn joins the Rochester-led national consortium testing whether reducing LINE-1 retrotransposon activity can preserve mobility, cognition, and vitality in aging.

title: What's New in Life Science, FemTech & Longevity -- February 2026
description: Monthly overview of biotech, healthtech, and longevity developments for February 2026, including FDA-cleared cellular reprogramming and AI diagnostics advances.

title: AgeXtend: AI Platform Predicts Geroprotective Potential of Compounds
description: AI-based multimodal geroprotector prediction platform leverages bioactivity data to predict geroprotective potential, assess toxicity, and identify target proteins.
